news.summary: A live webcast for the earnings call to discuss the financial results of the first quarter will be available on the corporate website on Tuesday, May 20, 2025. It is an opportunity for stakeholders to gain insights into the company's performance during the initial months of the year. Make sure to tune in to the webcast to learn about the key numbers and analysis from the first quarter.
news.headline: Reading International reports Q1 2025 results; earnings call webcast on May 20 to discuss financial performance.

news.summary: In May, AI-Media will be presenting their AI captioning and translation technology at two major events in Asia. They will be participating at KOBA in Booth #D326 and at Broadcast Asia in Booth #5I3-8. This technology aims to revolutionize accessibility by providing accurate captions and translations. These events will attract numerous industry professionals and offer a valuable opportunity for AI-Media to demonstrate their innovative solutions.
news.headline: AI-Media presents LEXI Voice at KOBA and Broadcast Asia 2025, booths #D326 and #5I3-8, exhibiting AI captioning and translation tech in Asia.

news.summary: Beijing, May 15, 2025 - The Shanghai Museum is gearing up for International Museum Day by planning a unique event. In a bid to set a world record, the museum will introduce a "Sleepless Night" mode for the upcoming On Top of the Pyramid: The Civilization of Ancient Egypt exhibition. This challenge aims to engage visitors in a new way by inviting them to experience the exhibition during extended hours without sleep. The initiative is part of the museum's efforts to promote cultural engagement and set a record for the longest continuous museum visit.
news.headline: Shanghai Museum announces "Sleepless Night" challenge for International Museum Day to engage Gen Zers, using digital magic.

news.summary: The S&P 500 experienced an increase as investor confidence rose due to diminishing expectations of a Federal Reserve interest rate cut, leading to a decrease in Treasury yields. This positive turn of events occurred on May 15, 2025, contributing to a notable upsurge in the stock market index.
news.headline: S&P 500 climbs as Fed-cut expectations lower treasury yields. Market update: 05/15/2025, source.com.

news.summary: Hackers using deepfake technology are targeting US federal and state officials in phishing campaigns, posing as senior government figures since April. The FBI warns of potential data theft if officials' accounts are compromised, allowing hackers to target others using acquired contact info. The scammers aim to access victims' accounts for sensitive data theft. In a separate incident, crypto founder Sandeep Narwal and others were impersonated in deepfake scams. Victims were directed to malicious platforms by hackers posing as them via deepfake audio calls. Vigilance is crucial to avoid falling victim, with recommendations to not install anything during online interactions initiated by others and to verify identities of contacts. The FBI advises checking for distorted features in images and videos, avoiding sharing sensitive info, clicking unknown links, and enabling two-factor authentication.
news.headline: FBI warns of AI scammers pretending to be US officials, stealing data & targeting crypto founders. Stay vigilant to avoid scams.

news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on May 15, 2025, Rubicon Organics Inc. (TSXV: ROMJ) (OTCQX: ROMJF) stated that it will release its financial report for the first quarter of 2025 on Tuesday, May 27, 2025, after the market closes. Rubicon Organics is a licensed producer specializing in the cultivation and sale of certified organic and premium cannabis products. The report will cover the period ending March 31, 2025, providing insights into the company's performance during this time.
news.headline: Rubicon Organics Inc. to report Q1 2025 results on May 27, 2025, after market close. TSXV: ROMJ, OTCQX: ROMJF.

news.summary: Analysts at JP Morgan predict that Bitcoin may outperform gold in the current year due to ongoing investments from institutions. The note was published on Thursday, suggesting a positive outlook for Bitcoin in comparison to the traditional precious metal. This development signifies a significant shift in investment trends, with Bitcoin potentially becoming a more favorable asset for institutional investors.
news.headline: JP Morgan predicts Bitcoin to surpass gold, as crypto derivatives market grows. Analysts foresee high institutional investments.

Users can now enjoy a better gaming experience with the latest additions and improvements on the platform.
news.headline: GeForce NOW expands library with 'DOOM: The Dark Ages' and more for premium members. Enhanced cloud gaming experience.

news.summary: On May 16, Executive Vice Chairman Chung Kisun engaged in official discussions with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer. The meeting focused on enhancing cooperation between Korea and the U.S. in the shipbuilding sector. HD Hyundai put forward a proposal for collaboration in port crane manufacturing. Chung emphasized the significance of U.S. shipbuilding, expressing a positive outlook on reinforcing ties in this realm.
news.headline: Hyundai's Vice Chairman met US Trade Rep. to discuss bilateral cooperation in shipbuilding, proposing collaboration on port crane manufacturing.

news.summary: Utah-based TruGolf Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: TRUG), a prominent provider of golf simulator technology, released its first quarter 2025 financial results. The company disclosed a revenue increase to $5.4 million, marking a 7.5% rise from the $5.0 million reported in the same period of 2024. However, net losses for the first quarter of 2025 doubled to ($2.6) million, compared to ($1.3) million in 2024, primarily due to interest expenses related to the conversion of convertible notes. In terms of earnings per share (EPS), the company saw an improvement to ($0.09) in the first quarter of 2025, up from ($0.22) in the corresponding period of 2024.
news.headline: TruGolf Q1 2025 sales increase by 7.5% to $5.4 million, but net losses double to ($2.6) million compared to ($1.3) million in Q1 2024. EPS improves to ($0.09) from ($0.22) in 2024.

news.summary: Federal prosecutors are pursuing a case against Tornado Cash founder Roman Storm but will drop a charge related to operating an unlicensed money transmitting business, aligning with the Department of Justice's recent rollback of crypto enforcement. The indictment will focus on Storm allegedly transmitting funds from criminal activities or to support illegal actions. Prosecutors claim Tornado Cash helped launder over $1 billion in crypto, including for the North Korean group Lazarus. Storm faces charges of money laundering conspiracy and violating US sanctions, each carrying up to 20 years in prison, and the unlicensed money transmitter conspiracy charge carries up to five years. Storm has pleaded not guilty, and his trial is set for July 14. Storm's associate Roman Semenov, believed to be in Russia, is also charged. Other crypto executives have cited the DOJ's shift in enforcement to seek dismissal of their charges, including Samourai Wallet's co-founders and SafeMoon's CEO.
news.headline: Tornado Cash founder's trial continues with minor adjustments due to DOJ policy change. $1 billion crypto laundered. Trial set for July 14.
